content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices, where the ithi^{th}ith vertex has color AiA_iAi​.

We say that an edge of this tree separates color CCC if, upon deleting this edge from the tree, **both** obtained components contain **at least one** vertex having color CCC.

The value of an edge is defined as the sum of all the colors it separates. For example, if an edge separates colors {1,4,7,13}\{1, 4, 7, 13\}{1,4,7,13}, then, its value is 1+4+7+13=251 + 4 + 7 + 13 = 251+4+7+13=25.

Find the value of every edge of the tree.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of vertices of the tree.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the colors of the vertices.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges of the tree. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers. The ithi^{th}ith of these integers should be the value of the ithi^{th}ith **input edge**.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.
